  var Module = typeof createMediapipeSolutionsPackedAssets !== 'undefined' ? createMediapipeSolutionsPackedAssets : {};

  if (!Module.expectedDataFileDownloads) {
    Module.expectedDataFileDownloads = 0;
  }

  Module.expectedDataFileDownloads++;
  (function() {
    // When running as a pthread, FS operations are proxied to the main thread, so we don't need to
    // fetch the .data bundle on the worker
    if (Module['ENVIRONMENT_IS_PTHREAD']) return;
    var loadPackage = function(metadata) {

      var PACKAGE_PATH = '';
      if (typeof window === 'object') {
        PACKAGE_PATH = window['encodeURIComponent'](window.location.pathname.toString().substring(0, window.location.pathname.toString().lastIndexOf('/')) + '/');
      } else if (typeof process === 'undefined' && typeof location !== 'undefined') {
        // web worker
        PACKAGE_PATH = encodeURIComponent(location.pathname.toString().substring(0, location.pathname.toString().lastIndexOf('/')) + '/');
      }
      var PACKAGE_NAME = 'blaze-out/k8-opt/genfiles/third_party/mediapipe/web/solutions/pose/pose_solution_packed_assets.data';
      var REMOTE_PACKAGE_BASE = 'pose_solution_packed_assets.data';
      if (typeof Module['locateFilePackage'] === 'function' && !Module['locateFile']) {
        Module['locateFile'] = Module['locateFilePackage'];
        err('warning: you defined Module.locateFilePackage, that has been renamed to Module.locateFile (using your locateFilePackage for now)');
      }
      var REMOTE_PACKAGE_NAME = Module['locateFile'] ? Module['locateFile'](REMOTE_PACKAGE_BASE, '') : REMOTE_PACKAGE_BASE;
var REMOTE_PACKAGE_SIZE = metadata['remote_package_size'];

      function fetchRemotePackage(packageName, packageSize, callback, errback) {
        if (typeof process === 'object' && typeof process.versions === 'object' && typeof process.versions.node === 'string') {
          require('fs').readFile(packageName, function(err, contents) {
            if (err) {
              errback(err);
            } else {
              callback(contents.buffer);
            }
          });
          return;
        }
        var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
        xhr.open('GET', packageName, true);
        xhr.responseType = 'arraybuffer';
        xhr.onprogress = function(event) {
          var url = packageName;
          var size = packageSize;
          if (event.total) size = event.total;
          if (event.loaded) {
            if (!xhr.addedTotal) {
              xhr.addedTotal = true;
              if (!Module.dataFileDownloads) Module.dataFileDownloads = {};
              Module.dataFileDownloads[url] = {
                loaded: event.loaded,
                total: size
              };
            } else {
              Module.dataFileDownloads[url].loaded = event.loaded;
            }
            var total = 0;
            var loaded = 0;
            var num = 0;
            for (var download in Module.dataFileDownloads) {
            var data = Module.dataFileDownloads[download];
              total += data.total;
              loaded += data.loaded;
              num++;
            }
            total = Math.ceil(total * Module.expectedDataFileDownloads/num);
            if (Module['setStatus']) Module['setStatus']('Downloading data... (' + loaded + '/' + total + ')');
          } else if (!Module.dataFileDownloads) {
            if (Module['setStatus']) Module['setStatus']('Downloading data...');
          }
        };
        xhr.onerror = function(event) {
          throw new Error("NetworkError for: " + packageName);
        }
        xhr.onload = function(event) {
          if (xhr.status == 200 || xhr.status == 304 || xhr.status == 206 || (xhr.status == 0 && xhr.response)) { // file URLs can return 0
            var packageData = xhr.response;
            callback(packageData);
          } else {
            throw new Error(xhr.statusText + " : " + xhr.responseURL);
          }
        };
        xhr.send(null);
      };

      function handleError(error) {
        console.error('package error:', error);
      };

      var fetchedCallback = null;
      var fetched = Module['getPreloadedPackage'] ? Module['getPreloadedPackage'](REMOTE_PACKAGE_NAME, REMOTE_PACKAGE_SIZE) : null;

      if (!fetched) fetchRemotePackage(REMOTE_PACKAGE_NAME, REMOTE_PACKAGE_SIZE, function(data) {
        if (fetchedCallback) {
          fetchedCallback(data);
          fetchedCallback = null;
        } else {
          fetched = data;
        }
      }, handleError);

    function runWithFS() {

      function assert(check, msg) {
        if (!check) throw msg + new Error().stack;
      }
Module['FS_createPath']("/", "third_party", true, true);
Module['FS_createPath']("/third_party", "mediapipe", true, true);
Module['FS_createPath']("/third_party/mediapipe", "modules", true, true);
Module['FS_createPath']("/third_party/mediapipe/modules", "pose_detection", true, true);

      /** @constructor */
      function DataRequest(start, end, audio) {
        this.start = start;
        this.end = end;
        this.audio = audio;
      }
      DataRequest.prototype = {
        requests: {},
        open: function(mode, name) {
          this.name = name;
          this.requests[name] = this;
          Module['addRunDependency']('fp ' + this.name);
        },
        send: function() {},
        onload: function() {
          var byteArray = this.byteArray.subarray(this.start, this.end);
          this.finish(byteArray);
        },
        finish: function(byteArray) {
          var that = this;
          
          Module['FS_createPreloadedFile'](this.name, null, byteArray, true, true, function() {
            Module['removeRunDependency']('fp ' + that.name);
          }, function() {
            if (that.audio) {
              Module['removeRunDependency']('fp ' + that.name); // workaround for chromium bug 124926 (still no audio with this, but at least we don't hang)
            } else {
              err('Preloading file ' + that.name + ' failed');
            }
          }, false, true); // canOwn this data in the filesystem, it is a slide into the heap that will never change

          this.requests[this.name] = null;
        }
      };

      var files = metadata['files'];
      for (var i = 0; i < files.length; ++i) {
        new DataRequest(files[i]['start'], files[i]['end'], files[i]['audio'] || 0).open('GET', files[i]['filename']);
      }

      function processPackageData(arrayBuffer) {
        assert(arrayBuffer, 'Loading data file failed.');
        assert(arrayBuffer.constructor.name === ArrayBuffer.name, 'bad input to processPackageData');
        var byteArray = new Uint8Array(arrayBuffer);
        var curr;
        // Reuse the bytearray from the XHR as the source for file reads.
          DataRequest.prototype.byteArray = byteArray;
          var files = metadata['files'];
          for (var i = 0; i < files.length; ++i) {
            DataRequest.prototype.requests[files[i].filename].onload();
          }          Module['removeRunDependency']('datafile_blaze-out/k8-opt/genfiles/third_party/mediapipe/web/solutions/pose/pose_solution_packed_assets.data');

      };
      Module['addRunDependency']('datafile_blaze-out/k8-opt/genfiles/third_party/mediapipe/web/solutions/pose/pose_solution_packed_assets.data');

      if (!Module.preloadResults) Module.preloadResults = {};

      Module.preloadResults[PACKAGE_NAME] = {fromCache: false};
      if (fetched) {
        processPackageData(fetched);
        fetched = null;
      } else {
        fetchedCallback = processPackageData;
      }

    }
    if (Module['calledRun']) {
      runWithFS();
    } else {
      if (!Module['preRun']) Module['preRun'] = [];
      Module["preRun"].push(runWithFS); // FS is not initialized yet, wait for it
    }

    }
    loadPackage({"files": [{"filename": "/third_party/mediapipe/modules/pose_detection/pose_detection.tflite", "start": 0, "end": 2962288}], "remote_package_size": 2962288});

__arraySum(array,index){var sum=0;for(var i=0;i<=index;sum+=array[i++]){}return sum}var __MONTH_DAYS_LEAP=[31,29,31,30,31,30,31,31,30,31,30,31];var __MONTH_DAYS_REGULAR=[31,28,31,30,31,30,31,31,30,31,30,31];function __addDays(date,days){var newDate=new Date(date.getTime());while(days>0){var leap=__isLeapYear(newDate.getFullYear());var MONTHS=["January","February","March","April","May","June","July","August","September","October","November","December"];function leadingSomething(value,digits,character){var str=typeof value=="number"?value.toString():value||"";while(str.length<digits){str=character[0]+str}return str}function leadingNulls(value,digits){return leadingSomething(value,digits,"0")}function compareByDay(date1,date2){function sgn(value){return value<0?-1:value>0?1:0}var compare;if((compare=sgn(date1.getFullYear()-date2.getFullYear()))===0){if((compare=sgn(date1.getMonth()-date2.getMonth()))===0){compare=sgn(date1.getDate()-date2.getDate())}}return compare}function getFirstWeekStartDate(janFourth){switch(janFourth.getDay()){case 0:return new Date(janFourth.getFullYear()-1,11,29);case 1:return janFourth;case 2:return new Date(janFourth.getFullYear(),0,3);case Date(janFourth.getFullYear()-1,11,30)}}function getWeekBasedYear(date){var thisDate=__addDays(new Date(date.tm_year+1900,0,1),date.tm_yday);var janFourthThisYear=new thisDate.getFullYear()+1}return thisDate.getFullYear()}return thisDate.getFullYear()-1}var EXPANSION_RULES_2={"%a":function(date){return days=date.tm_yday+7-(date.tm_wday+6)%7;return leadingNulls(Math.floor(days/7),2)},"%y":function(date){return(date.tm_year+1900).toString().substring(2)},"%Y":function(date){return date.tm_year+1900},"%z":function(date){var off=date.tm_gmtoff;var ahead=off>=0;off=Math.abs(off)/60;off=off/60*100+off%60;return(ahead?"+":"-")+String("0000"+off).slice(-4)},"%Z":function(date){return date.tm_zone},"%%":function(){return"%"}};pattern=pattern.replace(/%%/g,"\0\0");for(var rule in EXPANSION_RULES_2){if(pattern.includes(rule)){pattern=pattern.replace(new __arraySum(array,index){var sum=0;for(var i=0;i<=index;sum+=array[i++]){}return sum}var __MONTH_DAYS_LEAP=[31,29,31,30,31,30,31,31,30,31,30,31];var __MONTH_DAYS_REGULAR=[31,28,31,30,31,30,31,31,30,31,30,31];function __addDays(date,days){var newDate=new Date(date.getTime());while(days>0){var leap=__isLeapYear(newDate.getFullYear());var value=="number"?value.toString():value||"";while(str.length<digits){str=character[0]+str}return str}function leadingNulls(value,digits){return leadingSomething(value,digits,"0")}function compareByDay(date1,date2){function sgn(value){return value<0?-1:value>0?1:0}var getFirstWeekStartDate(janFourth){switch(janFourth.getDay()){case 0:return new Date(janFourth.getFullYear()-1,11,29);case 1:return janFourth;case 2:return new Date(janFourth.getFullYear(),0,3);case Date(janFourth.getFullYear()-1,11,30)}}function getWeekBasedYear(date){var thisDate=__addDays(new Date(date.tm_year+1900,0,1),date.tm_yday);var janFourthThisYear=new days=date.tm_yday+7-(date.tm_wday+6)%7;return leadingNulls(Math.floor(days/7),2)},"%y":function(date){return(date.tm_year+1900).toString().substring(2)},"%Y":function(date){return [squaring the\n      * uncertainty]\n      *\n      * The following polynomial approximates uncertainty alpha as a\n      * function of (p + 0.5):\n      */\n      const float c1 = 5.68842;\n float uncertainty =\n        1.0 - min(1.0, x * (c1 + x * (c2 + x * (c3 + x * (c4 + x * c5)))));\n\n      float outputValue = newMaskValue + (prevMaskValue - newMaskValue) *\n indexRatio;\n\n      var result = 0.0;\n\n      for (var i = 0; i < uniforms.realShape[1]; i = i + 1) {\n        // x = (-2|2 * PI / N) * index * i;\n        let x = exponentMultiplierTimesIndexRatio * f32(i);\n        let expR = cos(x);\n        let expI = sin(x);\n        let real = getReal(batch, i);\n        let imag = getImag(batch, i);\n\n        result = result +\n Gu{constructor(e){this.variableNames=["x","indices"],this.workgroupSize=[256,1,1],this.size=!0,this.outputShape=e,this.dispatchLayout=Ht(this.outputShape),this.dispatch=Wt(this.dispatchLayout,this.outputShape,this.workgroupSize),this.uniforms="inputSize : i32, firstPass : i32, negativeInf : f32,\n        dir : i32, inc : i32,",this.shaderKey="swap"}getUserCode(){return`\n        ${_t("index")} {\n          if (index < uniforms.size) {\n            let outC = getCoordsFromIndex(index);\n            let batch = outC[0];\n            let elemIdx = outC[1];\n            // We compare elements pair-wise within a group of size 2 * inc.\n            // The comparing rule for each group alternates between ascending\n            // and descending. Within each group, we compare each pair at\n            // positions i and i+inc. To decide whether an element at position i\n            // is x0 or x1, we mod it by 2 * inc, if the result is smaller than\n            // inc, it is in the first half of the group, we denote it as x0,\n            // otherwise we denote it as x1.\n            // For example, as shown in the Bitonic top K paper referenced\n            // above, Figure5(a) shows that element[1] is in the second half of\n            // the group when group size is 2, but it is in the first half of\n            // the group when group size is 4.\n            let isFirstInPair = elemIdx % (2 * uniforms.inc) < uniforms.inc;\n            var i = 0;\n            if (isFirstInPair) {\n              i = elemIdx;\n            } else {\n              i = elemIdx - uniforms.inc;\n            }\n\n            var i0 = 0;\n            if (uniforms.firstPass == 1) {\n              i0 = i;\n            } else {\n              i0 = i32(getIndices(batch, i));\n            }\n\n            var i1 = 0;\n            if (uniforms.firstPass == 1) {\n              i1 = i + uniforms.inc;\n            } else {\n              i1 = i32(getIndices(batch, i + uniforms.inc));\n            }\n\n            var x0 = f32(0.0);\n            var x1 = f32(0.0);\n            if (i0 < uniforms.inputSize) {\n              x0 = getX(batch, i0);\n            } else {\n              x0 = uniforms.negativeInf;\n            }\n            if (i1 < uniforms.inputSize) {\n              x1 = getX(batch, i1);\n            } else {\n              x1 = uniforms.negativeInf;\n            }\n\n            let reverse = elemIdx % (2 * uniforms.dir) >= uniforms.dir;\n            let isGreater = x0 > x1 || (x0 == x1 && i1 > i0);\n            if (reverse == isGreater) {\n              // Elements in opposite order of direction\n              let iTemp = i0;\n              i0 = i1;\n              i1 = iTemp;\n            }\n            if (isFirstInPair) {\n              setOutputAtIndex(index, f32(i0));\n            } else {\n              setOutputAtIndex(index, f32(i1));\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      `}}class Hu{constructor(e){this.variableNames=["x","indices"],this.workgroupSize=[256,1,1],this.size=!0,this.outputShape=e,this.dispatchLayout=Ht(this.outputShape),this.dispatch=Wt(this.dispatchLayout,this.outputShape,this.workgroupSize),this.uniforms="inputSize : i32, firstPass : i32, k : i32,",this.shaderKey="merge"}getUserCode(){return`\n        ${_t("index")} {\n          if (index < uniforms.size) {\n            let outC = getCoordsFromIndex(index);\n            let batch = outC[0];\n            let elemIdx = outC[1];\n            // The output size is half of the previous size.\n            // If the previous sequence is | | | | _ _ _ _  | | | |  _ _ _ _\n            // (k=4), we only need to output the indices at positions |, the\n            // indices at positions _ can be thrown away, see Figure5(b) After\n            // Phase 2 (Merge phase) in the Bitonic Top K paper referenced\n            // above.\n            // For example, the paper shows we only need to output the orange\n            // bars. The output sequence should look like this | | | | | | | |.\n            // Because the sequence is halved, to map the output index back to\n            // the previous sequence to find the corresponding value, we need\n            // to double the index. When we double the index, we basically\n            // interpolate a position, so 2i looks like\n            // | _ | _ | _ | _ | _ | _ | _. We move the | to the first k\n            // position of each 2k positions by - elemIdx % k. E.g. for output\n            // at index 4,5,6,7, we want to get the corresponding element at\n            // original index 8,9,10,11, for output at index 8,9,10,11,\n            // we want to get the corresponding element at original index\n            // 16,17,18,19, so on and so forth.\n\n            var i = 0;\n            if (elemIdx < uniforms.k) {\n              i = elemIdx;\n            } else {\n              i = elemIdx * 2 - elemIdx % uniforms.k;\n            }\n            var i0 = 0;\n            if (uniforms.firstPass == 1) {\n              i0 = i;\n            } else {\n              i0 = i32(getIndices(batch, i));\n            }\n            var i1 = 0;\n            if (uniforms.firstPass == 1) {\n              i1 = i + uniforms.k;\n            } else {\n              i1 = i32(getIndices(batch, i + uniforms.k));\n            }\n\n            let x0 = getX(batch, i0);\n            var x1 = f32(0.0);\n            if (i1 < uniforms.inputSize) {\n              x1 = getX(batch, i1);\n            } else {\n              x1 = x0;\n            }\n\n            if (x0 >= x1) {\n              setOutputAtIndex(index, f32(i0));\n            } else {\n              setOutputAtIndex(index, f32(i1));\n            }\n          }\n        }\n      `}}function qu(e,t){null!==t&&e.disposeData(t.dataId)}function Ku(e){let t=1;for(;t<e;)t*=2;return t}const ju{constructor(e){this.variableNames=["Image","Transforms"],this.uniforms="interpolationModeId : i32, fillModeId : i32, fillValue : f32,",this.workgroupSize=[64,1,1],this.size=!0,this.outputShape=e,this.dispatchLayout=Ht(this.outputShape),this.dispatch=Wt(this.dispatchLayout,this.outputShape,this.workgroupSize),this.shaderKey="transform"}getUserCode(){return`\n          fn mapCoord(outCoord : f32, len : f32) -> f32{\n            var inCoord = outCoord;\n            if(uniforms.fillModeId == 2) {\n              if (inCoord < 0.0) {\n                if (len <= 1.0) {\n                  inCoord = 0.0;\n                } else {\n                  let sz2 = 2.0 * len;\n                  if (inCoord < sz2) {\n                    inCoord = sz2 * f32(i32(f32(-inCoord / sz2))) +\n                    inCoord;\n                  }\n                  if (inCoord < -len) {\n                    inCoord = inCoord + sz2;\n                  } else {\n                    inCoord = -inCoord - 1.0;\n                  }\n                }\n              } else if (inCoord > len - 1.0) {\n                if (len <= 1.0) {\n                  inCoord = 0.0;\n                } else {\n                  let sz2 = 2.0 * len;\n                  inCoord = inCoord - sz2 * f32(i32(f32(inCoord / sz2)));\n                  if (inCoord >= len) {\n                    inCoord = sz2 - inCoord - 1.0;\n                  }\n                }\n              }\n              return clamp(inCoord, 0.0, len - 1.0);\n            } else if (uniforms.fillModeId == 3) {\n              if (inCoord < 0.0) {\n                if (len <= 1.0) {\n                  inCoord = 0.0;\n                } else {\n                  let sz = len - 1.0;\n                  inCoord = inCoord + len * (f32(i32(f32(-inCoord / sz))) + 1.0);\n                }\n              } else if (inCoord > len - 1.0) {\n                if (len <= 1.0) {\n                  inCoord = 0.0;\n                } else {\n                  let sz = len - 1.0;\n                  inCoord = inCoord - len * f32(i32(f32(inCoord / sz)));\n                }\n              }\n              return clamp(inCoord, 0.0, len - 1.0);\n            } else if (uniforms.fillModeId == 4) {\n              return clamp(outCoord, 0.0, len - 1.0);\n            }\n            return outCoord;\n          }\n          fn readWithFillValue(batch : i32, coordY : i32, coordX : i32,\n            channel : i32) -> f32 {\n            var outputValue : f32;\n            if (0 <= coordY && coordY < uniforms.imageShape[1] && 0 <= coordX && coordX < uniforms.imageShape[2]) {\n                outputValue = getImage(batch, coordY, coordX, channel);\n            } else {\n              outputValue = uniforms.fillValue;\n            }\n            return outputValue;\n          }\n\n          ${_t("index")} {\n            if (index < uniforms.size) {\n              let coords = getCoordsFromIndex(index);\n              var outputValue : f32;\n              let batch = coords[0];\n              let x = coords[2];\n              let y = coords[1];\n              let channel = coords[3];\n              let xf = f32(x);\n              let yf = f32(y);\n              let a1 = getTransforms(batch, 0);\n              let a2 = getTransforms(batch, 1);\n              let a3 = getTransforms(batch, 2);\n              let b1 = getTransforms(batch, 3);\n              let b2 = getTransforms(batch, 4);\n              let b3 = getTransforms(batch, 5);\n              let c1 = getTransforms(batch, 6);\n              let c2 = getTransforms(batch, 7);\n              let projection = c1 * xf + c2 * yf + 1.0;\n              if (projection == 0.0) {\n                outputValue = uniforms.fillValue;\n              } else {\n                let inX = (a1 * xf + a2 * yf + a3) / projection;\n                let inY = (b1 * xf + b2 * yf + b3) / projection;\n                let mapX = mapCoord(inX, f32(uniforms.imageShape[2]));\n                let mapY = mapCoord(inY, f32(uniforms.imageShape[1]));\n\n                if (uniforms.interpolationModeId == 1) {\n                  let coordY = i32(round(mapY));\n                  let coordX = i32(round(mapX));\n                  outputValue = readWithFillValue(batch, coordY, coordX,\n                    channel);\n                } else {\n                  let yFloor = floor(mapY);\n                  let xFloor = floor(mapX);\n                  let yCeil = yFloor + 1.0;\n                  let xCeil = xFloor + 1.0;\n e(t){l(this,e),this.variableNames=["x","indices"],this.customUniforms=[{name:"n",type:"int"},{name:"firstPass",type:"int"},{name:"negativeInf",type:"float"},{name:"dir",type:"int"},{name:"inc",type:"int"}],this.outputShape=t,this.userCode="\n       void main() {\n         ivec2 coords = getOutputCoords();\n         int batch = coords[0];\n         int elemIdx = coords[1];\n\n         // We compare elements pair-wise within a group of size 2 * inc.\n         // The comparing rule for each group alternates between ascending\n         // and descending. Within each group, we compare each pair at\n         // positions i and i+inc. To decide whether an element at position i\n         // is x0 or x1, we mod it by 2 * inc, if the result is smaller than\n         // inc, it is in the first half of the group, we denote it as x0,\n         // otherwise we denote it as x1.\n         // For example, as shown in the Bitonic top K paper referenced above,\n         // Figure5(a) shows that element[1] is in the\n         // second half of the group when group size is 2, but it is in the\n         // first half of the group when group size is 4.\n\n         bool isFirstInPair = imod(elemIdx, 2 * inc) < inc;\n         int i = isFirstInPair ? elemIdx : elemIdx - inc;\n\n         int i0 = firstPass == 1 ? i : int(getIndices(batch, i));\n         int i1 = firstPass == 1 ? i + inc : int(getIndices(batch, i + inc));\n         float x0 = i0 < n ? getX(batch, i0) : negativeInf;\n         float x1 = i1 < n ? getX(batch, i1) : negativeInf;\n\n         // Denotes which direction indices are in (ascending or descending).\n         bool reverse = imod(elemIdx, 2 * dir) >= dir;\n         bool isGreater = x0 > x1 || (x0 == x1 && i1 > i0);\n         if (reverse == isGreater) { // Elements in opposite order of direction\n           int iTemp = i0;\n           i0 = i1;\n           i1 = iTemp;\n         }\n         if (isFirstInPair) {\n            setOutput(float(i0));\n         } else {\n            setOutput(float(i1));\n         }\n       }\n     "})),jFe=p((function e(t){l(this,e),this.variableNames=["x","indices"],this.customUniforms=[{name:"n",type:"int"},{name:"firstPass",type:"int"},{name:"k",type:"int"}],this.outputShape=t,this.userCode="\n    void main() {\n         // Takes max of indices (0, k), (1, k + 1), (2, k + 2) ...\n         ivec2 coords = getOutputCoords();\n         int batch = coords[0];\n         int elemIdx = coords[1];\n\n         // The output size is half of the previous size.\n         // If the previous sequence is | | | | _ _ _ _  | | | |  _ _ _ _ (k=4),\n         // we only need to output the indices at positions |, the indices at\n         // positions _ can be thrown away, see Figure5(b) After Phase 2\n         // (Merge phase) in the Bitonic Top K paper referenced above.\n         // For example, the paper shows we only need to output the orange bars.\n         // The output sequence should look like this | | | | | | | |.\n         // Because the sequence is halved, to map the output index back\n         // to the previous sequence to find the corresponding value,\n         // we need to double the index. When we double the index,\n         // we basically interpolate a position, so 2i looks like\n         // | _ | _ | _ | _ | _ | _ | _. We move the | to the first k position\n         // of each 2k positions by - elemIdx % k. E.g. for output at\n         // index 4,5,6,7, we want to get the corresponding element at\n         // original index 8,9,10,11, for output at index 8,9,10,11,\n         // we want to get the corresponding element at original index\n         // 16,17,18,19, so on and so forth.\n\n         int i = elemIdx < k ? elemIdx : (elemIdx * 2 - imod(elemIdx, k));\n         int i0 = firstPass == 1 ?       <strong>Connecting to Supabase Auth</strong>
      <span id="statusText">Please wait while we complete the OAuth callback.</span>
    </div>
    <div class="actions">
      <a class="btn" href="./login.html">Back to sign in</a>
      <a class="btn" href="./index.html">Homepage</a>
    </div>
  </section>

  <script src="./public-app-config.js"></script>
  <script src="/api/runtime-config.js"></script>
  <script src="./app-data.js"></script>
  <script>
    const store = window.SmartSwingStore;
    const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');
    const statusBox = document.getElementById('statusBox');

    async function completeAuth() {
      if (!store.isSupabaseConfigured()) {
        statusBox.querySelector('strong').textContent = 'Supabase public config is missing';
        statusText.textContent = 'Add the production Supabase URL and anon key before using Google, Apple, or Meta login.';
        return;
      }

      try {
        const user = await store.restoreSupabaseSession();
        if (!user) {
          window.location.href = './login.html?type=error&message=' + encodeURIComponent('OAuth session was not completed. Please try again.');
          return;
        }
        statusBox.querySelector('strong').textContent = 'Authentication complete';
        statusText.textContent = 'Redirecting to your SmartSwing workspace...';
        const destination = store.getPostAuthDestinationForUser(user);
        setTimeout(() => { window.location.href = destination; }, 450);
      } catch (error) {
        window.location.href = './login.html?type=error&message=' + encodeURIComponent(error.message || 'OAuth sign-in failed.');
      }
    }

    completeAuth();
  </script>
</body>
</html>

        <p style="margin-top:14px"><a class="btn-secondary" href="#serve-analysis">Read article</a></p>
      </article>

        <div class="eyebrow">Forehand</div>

        <p>The high-cost forehand patterns that should be corrected early, plus how drills should map back to the report.</p>
        <p style="margin-top:14px"><a class="btn-secondary" href="#forehand-mistakes">Read article</a></p>
      </article>
      <article class="story-card">
        <div class="eyebrow">Improvement</div>
        <h3 style="margin-top:14px">USTA rating improvement guide</h3>
        <p>Why better players practice for transfer, not for drill comfort, and how video review can tighten that loop.</p>
        <p style="margin-top:14px"><a class="btn-secondary" href="#usta-guide">Read article</a></p>
      </article>
    </section>

    <section class="content-grid" id="serve-analysis">
      <article class="panel section-stack" style="grid-column: 1 / -1;">
        <div class="eyebrow">Article 1</div>
        <h2>How to analyze your tennis serve at home</h2>
        <p>The easiest way to waste a serve review is to film from the wrong place and then expect perfect answers. Start with a stable phone or camera positioned behind the baseline or from a side angle that clearly shows loading, toss, contact, and follow-through. You do not need a professional setup. You need a clean view and enough repetition to show a pattern.</p>
        <p>Before you upload, decide what you want answered. Are you missing first serves long? Losing rhythm under pressure? Struggling to generate pace? Context shapes how you read the result. SmartSwing is strongest when the player knows the goal, because the report can then be interpreted against something concrete instead of vague curiosity.</p>
        <p>Once the analysis comes back, ignore the temptation to chase everything. A good report should narrow you toward the highest-cost issue first. If the serve chain is breaking because the load is shallow and the contact point is late, that matters more than polishing lower-impact details. Take one technical priority, one drill, and one tactical adjustment into the next session. That is where improvement starts to feel real.</p>
      </article>
    </section>

    <section class="content-grid" id="forehand-mistakes">
      <article class="panel section-stack" style="grid-column: 1 / -1;">
        <div class="eyebrow">Article 2</div>
        <h2>5 forehand mistakes AI can spot before they become habits</h2>
        <p>Forehand problems usually show up in clusters, not as one isolated flaw. The common ones are rushed spacing, a late unit turn, unstable contact height, weak loading into the outside leg, and a finish that does not match the intention of the ball. Left alone, those habits get reinforced because players often compensate just enough to stay functional in practice.</p>
        <p>That is where AI feedback is useful. It helps identify which movement pattern is actually producing the miss instead of forcing the player to guess from feel alone. If the contact is consistently late, the solution might not be a generic â€œswing earlier.â€ It might be footwork, spacing, or preparation timing. Better diagnosis leads to better drill selection.</p>
        <p>The real test is whether the recommendation creates transfer. If the drill does not produce a change in live rally behavior, it is probably too disconnected from the problem. SmartSwingâ€™s value is highest when the drill, the report, and the playerâ€™s tactical intention all reinforce the same fix.</p>
      </article>
    </section>

    <section class="content-grid" id="usta-guide">
      <article class="panel section-stack" style="grid-column: 1 / -1;">
        <div class="eyebrow">Article 3</div>
        <h2>USTA rating improvement guide: how to practice with more transfer</h2>
        <p>Most players trying to climb ratings do too much comfortable practice and not enough targeted correction. Rating jumps usually come from stronger decision quality, cleaner technique under pressure, and more reliable patterns on the balls that show up most in matches. That means practice has to connect to match transfer, not just repetition volume.</p>
        <p>Use video and analysis to find the narrowest issue with the biggest competitive cost. Maybe you are losing short balls because your balance is poor moving forward. Maybe your second-serve pattern is predictable. Maybe you are neutralizing your own forehand with weak spacing. Once the issue is clear, the right drill block becomes easier to design and easier to stick with.</p>
        <p>The point of technology is not to replace coaching judgment. It is to reduce wasted reps. If a player can leave every review knowing what changed, why it matters, and what to work on next, rating improvement becomes a more disciplined process instead of a hopeful one.</p>
      </article>
    </section>

    <section class="panel cta-band">
      <div>
        <div class="eyebrow">Try the workflow</div>
        <h2 style="margin-top:16px">Read one article, then run one analysis.</h2>
        <p>The fastest way to judge the product is to compare the education layer with the actual report flow.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="cta-actions">
        <a class="btn-primary" href="./analyze.html">Run Analysis</a>
        <a class="btn-secondary" href="./pricing.html">See Pricing</a>
        <a class="btn-secondary" href="./contact.html">Contact</a>
      </div>
    </section>

    <footer class="legal-footer">

        <p>Publishing tennis analysis, practice, and player-development content on the new main site.</p>
      </div>

  <div class="page">
    <header class="top">
      <a class="brand" href="./index.html">Smart<span>Swing</span> AI</a>
      <div style="display:flex;gap:10px;flex-wrap:wrap">

    <section class="grid">
      <article class="panel">
        <div class="eyebrow">Your cart</div>
        <h1>Your cart</h1>
        <p class="muted">This review step keeps the selected plan, billing cadence, entitlement notes, and trial state visible before checkout. Paid plans hand off to Stripe-hosted recurring billing only after you confirm the order.</p>
        <div class="actions">
          <a class="btn" href="./pricing.html">Change plan</a>
          <a class="btn-primary" id="checkoutBtn" href="./checkout.html">Continue to checkout</a>
        </div>
      </article>

      <article class="panel">
        <h2 id="planTitle">Selected plan</h2>
        <div class="list" id="cartDetails"></div>
      </article>
    </section>
  </div>

  <script src="./public-app-config.js"></script>
  <script src="/api/runtime-config.js"></script>
  <script src="./app-data.js"></script>
  <script>
    const store = window.SmartSwingStore;
    const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    const planId = params.get('plan') || 'pro';
    const billingInterval = store.normalizeBillingInterval(params.get('interval') || 'monthly');
    const plan = store.PLAN_DEFINITIONS[planId] || store.PLAN_DEFINITIONS.pro;
    const user = store.getCurrentUser();
    const trialEligibility = store.getTrialEligibility(user?.id, planId);
    const planPrice = store.getPlanPrice(planId, billingInterval);
    store.clearCheckoutIntent?.();
    document.getElementById('planTitle').textContent = planId === 'free'
      ? `${plan.name} | $0/month`
      : `${plan.name} | $${planPrice.amount.toFixed(2)}/${billingInterval === 'yearly' ? 'year' : 'month'}`;
    document.getElementById('checkoutBtn').href = `./checkout.html?plan=${encodeURIComponent(planId)}&interval=${encodeURIComponent(billingInterval)}`;
    document.getElementById('cartDetails').innerHTML = [
      `Billing: ${planId === 'free' ? 'Free activation' : (billingInterval === 'yearly' ? `Yearly billing at $${planPrice.amount.toFixed(2)} | monthly equivalent $${planPrice.monthlyEquivalent.toFixed(2)}` : `Monthly billing at $${planPrice.amount.toFixed(2)}`)}`,
      `Monthly reviews: ${Number.isFinite(plan.monthlyReviews) ? plan.monthlyReviews : 'Unlimited'}`,
      `Trial status: ${plan.id === 'free' ? 'No trial required' : (trialEligibility.eligible ? 'Eligible for current account' : (trialEligibility.reason || 'Not available'))}`,
      `Included perks: ${plan.perks.join(' | ')}`,
      `Account: ${user?.email || 'Sign in required before checkout'}`,
      `Upgrade reason: ${plan.id === 'free' ? 'Start with zero friction.' : (plan.id === 'starter' ? 'More volume plus saved reports.' : (plan.id === 'pro' ? 'Unlimited review flow and full library access.' : 'Highest support layer with coach workflow and tournament prep.'))}`
    ].map((item) => `<div class="item">${item}</div>`).join('');
  </script>
</body>
</html>

        <h1>Confirm your plan with a checkout flow that feels trustworthy.</h1>
        <p>SmartSwing keeps plan details, billing cadence, and account context visible here. The actual recurring payment is completed on Stripe-hosted subscription checkout so card handling, wallets, and subscription security stay in Stripe's controlled flow.</p>
        <div class="toggle" id="billingToggle">
          <button type="button" data-interval="monthly">Monthly</button>

  <script src="./public-app-config.js"></script>
  <script src="/api/runtime-config.js"></script>
  <script src="./app-data.js"></script>
  <script>
    const store = window.SmartSwingStore;
    const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
    const planId = params.get('plan') || 'pro';
    let billingInterval = store.normalizeBillingInterval(params.get('interval') || 'monthly');
    const plan = store.PLAN_DEFINITIONS[planId] || store.PLAN_DEFINITIONS.pro;
    const activeUser = store.getCurrentUser();
    const trialEligibility = store.getTrialEligibility(activeUser?.id, planId);

    const checkoutFlow = document.getElementById('checkoutFlow');
    const checkoutHeading = document.getElementById('checkoutHeading');
    document.getElementById('backToCart').href = `./cart.html?plan=${encodeURIComponent(planId)}&interval=${encodeURIComponent(billingInterval)}`;

    function renderAccountCard() {
      const accountLabel = document.getElementById('accountLabel');
      const accountCopy = document.getElementById('accountCopy');
      if (!activeUser) {
        accountLabel.textContent = 'Account required';
        accountCopy.textContent = 'Create or sign into an account before the final Stripe step.';
        return;
      }
      accountLabel.textContent = activeUser.fullName || activeUser.email;
      accountCopy.textContent = `${activeUser.email} | ${activeUser.country || 'Location not yet added'} | ${plan.name} checkout`;
    }

    function renderSummary() {
      const price = store.getPlanPrice(planId, billingInterval);
      document.getElementById('summaryTitle').textContent = planId === 'free'
        ? `${plan.name} | $0/month`
        : `${plan.name} | $${price.amount.toFixed(2)}/${billingInterval === 'yearly' ? 'year' : 'month'}`;
      document.getElementById('summaryList').innerHTML = [
        { title: 'Plan', body: plan.name },
        { title: 'Billing cadence', body: plan.id === 'free' ? 'Free activation' : (billingInterval === 'yearly' ? `Yearly billing | save 15% | monthly equivalent $${price.monthlyEquivalent.toFixed(2)}` : 'Monthly billing | cancel anytime') },
        { title: 'Monthly reviews', body: Number.isFinite(plan.monthlyReviews) ? `${plan.monthlyReviews}` : 'Unlimited' },
        { title: 'Trial status', body: plan.id === 'free' ? 'No trial required' : (trialEligibility.eligible ? 'Eligible if enabled for this account' : (trialEligibility.reason || 'Unavailable')) },
        { title: 'Provider', body: plan.id === 'free' ? 'SmartSwing internal activation' : 'Stripe secure recurring checkout' },
        { title: 'Included perks', body: plan.perks.join(' | ') }
      ].map((item) => `<div class="summary-card"><strong>${item.title}</strong>${item.body}</div>`).join('');
    }

    function renderCheckoutFlow() {
      const checkoutRoute = store.getExternalCheckoutRoute(planId, {
        billingInterval,
        source: 'checkout-page',
        returnTo: './payment-success.html',
        cancelTo: './payment-cancelled.html'
      });
      if (planId === 'free') {
        checkoutHeading.textContent = 'Activate free access';
        checkoutFlow.innerHTML = `
          <div class="summary-card">
            <strong>Free access activates inside SmartSwing</strong>
            One free analysis per month becomes available immediately after activation.
          </div>
          <button class="btn-primary" id="submitBtn" type="submit">Activate free plan</button>
          <div class="micro">No payment details are required for the free plan.</div>
        `;
        return;
      }

      checkoutHeading.textContent = `Continue to Stripe checkout | ${billingInterval === 'yearly' ? 'yearly billing' : 'monthly billing'}`;
      checkoutFlow.innerHTML = checkoutRoute.ready ? `
        <div class="summary-card">
          <strong>Recurring billing is hosted by Stripe</strong>
          You will leave SmartSwing briefly to finish the official subscription checkout, then return for payment verification and onboarding.
        </div>
        <button class="btn-primary" id="submitBtn" type="submit">Continue to Stripe checkout</button>
        <div class="micro">Final method availability can vary by country, browser, wallet setup, and Stripe configuration.</div>
      ` : `
        <div class="summary-card">
          <strong>Stripe checkout is not fully configured yet</strong>
          The checkout UI is ready, but one or more Stripe keys or price ids are still missing.
        </div>
        <button class="btn" id="submitBtn" type="submit">Review setup details</button>
        <div class="micro">${checkoutRoute.reason}</div>
      `;
    }

    function syncToggle() {
      document.querySelectorAll('#billingToggle [data-interval]').forEach((button) => {
        button.classList.toggle('active', button.dataset.interval === billingInterval);
      });
      document.getElementById('backToCart').href = `./cart.html?plan=${encodeURIComponent(planId)}&interval=${encodeURIComponent(billingInterval)}`;
    }

    document.querySelectorAll('#billingToggle [data-interval]').forEach((button) => {
      button.addEventListener('click', () => {
        billingInterval = store.normalizeBillingInterval(button.dataset.interval);
        const nextUrl = new URL(window.location.href);
        nextUrl.searchParams.set('interval', billingInterval);
        window.history.replaceState({}, '', nextUrl);
        renderSummary();
        renderCheckoutFlow();
        syncToggle();
      });
    });

    renderAccountCard();
    renderSummary();
    renderCheckoutFlow();
    syncToggle();

    document.getElementById('checkoutForm').addEventListener('submit', async (event) => {
      event.preventDefault();
      const user = store.getCurrentUser();
      const checkoutRoute = store.getExternalCheckoutRoute(planId, {
        billingInterval,
        source: 'checkout-page',
        returnTo: './payment-success.html',
        cancelTo: './payment-cancelled.html'
      });
      if (!user) {
        store.setCheckoutIntent?.({
          planId,
          billingInterval,
          source: 'checkout-page'
        });
        window.location.href = `./signup.html?plan=${encodeURIComponent(planId)}${planId !== 'free' ? `&interval=${encodeURIComponent(billingInterval)}` : ''}`;
        return;
      }

      if (planId === 'free') {
        try {
          store.setCurrentPlan('free', user.id);
        } catch (error) {
          alert(error.message || 'Unable to activate the free plan.');
          return;
        }
        window.location.href = './welcome.html?plan=free';
        return;
      }

      if (!checkoutRoute.ready) {
        window.location.href = './payment-success.html?provider=stripe&status=setup-required&plan=' + encodeURIComponent(planId);
        return;
      }

      const submitBtn = document.getElementById('submitBtn');
      submitBtn.setAttribute('disabled', 'disabled');
      submitBtn.textContent = 'Opening Stripe...';

      try {
        const session = await store.createStripeCheckout(planId, {
          billingInterval,
          source: 'checkout-page',
          returnTo: './payment-success.html',
          cancelTo: './payment-cancelled.html'
        });
        window.location.href = session.url;
      } catch (error) {
        submitBtn.removeAttribute('disabled');
        submitBtn.textContent = 'Continue to Stripe checkout';
        alert(error.message || 'Unable to start secure checkout right now.');
      }
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

    <section class="hero">

        <div class="eyebrow">Coach Command Layer</div>
        <h1>Review athletes by tab, not by overload.</h1>
        <p class="muted">Messaging, schedule, athlete analytics, and exports now live in separate views so coaches can focus faster and manage cleaner accountability loops.</p>
        <div class="stats" id="coachStats"></div>
      </article>
      <article class="hero-art"><div class="card"><div class="mini">Right now</div><h3>Start with the highest-risk athlete.</h3><p class="muted" id="coachHeroCopy">Use the athlete tab to open the top issue, then send one clear coaching priority.</p></div></article>
    </section>

    <section class="tabs">
      <button class="tab active" data-tab="overview">Overview</button>
      <button class="tab" data-tab="athletes">Athletes</button>
      <button class="tab" data-tab="schedule">Scheduling</button>
      <button class="tab" data-tab="messages">Messaging</button>
      <button class="tab" data-tab="analytics">Analytics</button>
      <button class="tab" data-tab="exports">Exports</button>
    </section>

    <section class="tab-panel active" id="tab-overview">
      <div class="grid">

  <script>
    const store = window.SmartSwingStore;
    const access = store.getAccessContext();
    if (!access.user || !access.canAccessCoachDashboard) window.location.href = './login.html';

    const summary = store.getCoachSummary();
    const athletes = summary.athletes || [];
    const sessions = store.getVisibleCoachSessionsForCurrentUser();
    const messages = store.getVisibleMessagesForCurrentUser();

    document.querySelectorAll('.tab').forEach((button) => {
      button.addEventListener('click', () => {
        document.querySelectorAll('.tab').forEach((el) => el.classList.remove('active'));
        document.querySelectorAll('.tab-panel').forEach((el) => el.classList.remove('active'));
        button.classList.add('active');
        document.getElementById(`tab-${button.dataset.tab}`).classList.add('active');
      });
    });

    document.getElementById('coachName').textContent = access.user.fullName || 'Coach';
    document.getElementById('coachMeta').textContent = `${summary.users?.length || 0} athletes | ${summary.urgentAthletes || 0} urgent`;
    document.getElementById('coachHeroCopy').textContent = `${summary.urgentAthletes || 0} athletes need immediate attention based on stale tracking or large mechanical gaps.`;
    const coachAvatar = document.getElementById('coachAvatar');
    coachAvatar.textContent = (access.user.fullName || 'Coach').charAt(0).toUpperCase();
    if (access.user.avatarDataUrl) {
      coachAvatar.style.backgroundImage = `url(${access.user.avatarDataUrl})`;
      coachAvatar.textContent = '';
    }

    document.getElementById('coachStats').innerHTML = [
      ['Athletes', summary.users?.length || 0],
      ['Urgent', summary.urgentAthletes || 0],
      ['Sessions', sessions.length],
      ['Assessments', summary.assessments?.length || 0]
    ].map(([label, value]) => `<div class="card"><div class="mini">${label}</div><h3>${value}</h3></div>`).join('');

    document.getElementById('priorityQueue').innerHTML = athletes.slice(0, 6).map((item, index) => `<details class="detail-card" ${index === 0 ? 'open' : ''}><summary><div><div class="mini">Priority ${index + 1}</div><h3>${item.user.fullName}</h3></div><div class="pill">${item.latest?.overallScore || 0}%</div></summary><div class="detail-body">${item.topIssue ? `${item.topIssue.metric} | ${item.topIssue.delta > 0 ? '+' : ''}${item.topIssue.delta} deg` : 'Needs new assessment'}. Open this athlete to decide the first correction note and next assignment.</div></details>`).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">No athlete data yet.</p></div>';
    document.getElementById('coachSessions').innerHTML = sessions.slice(0, 6).map((item) => {
      const athlete = store.getUsers().find((user) => user.id === item.userId);
      return `<div class="card"><div class="row"><div><h3>${athlete?.fullName || 'Athlete session'}</h3><p class="muted">${item.focus} | ${item.format}</p></div><div class="pill">${new Date(item.when).toLocaleString()}</div></div></div>`;
    }).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">No coach sessions scheduled.</p></div>';
    document.getElementById('athleteRoster').innerHTML = athletes.map((item, index) => `<details class="detail-card" ${index === 0 ? 'open' : ''}><summary><div><h3>${item.user.fullName}</h3><p class="muted">${item.latest ? `${item.latest.shotType} | ${item.latest.overallScore}%` : 'No assessment yet'}</p></div><div class="pill">${item.retention.accountabilityState || 'on-track'}</div></summary><div class="detail-body">${item.goals?.length || 0} goals | ${item.drills?.length || 0} drills | ${item.nextSession ? `Next session ${new Date(item.nextSession.when).toLocaleDateString()}` : 'No upcoming session'}.</div></details>`).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">No athletes found.</p></div>';
    document.getElementById('scheduleList').innerHTML = sessions.map((item) => {
      const athlete = store.getUsers().find((user) => user.id === item.userId);
      return `<div class="card"><div class="row"><div><h3>${athlete?.fullName || 'Athlete session'}</h3><p class="muted">${item.focus}</p></div><div class="pill">${new Date(item.when).toLocaleString()}</div></div></div>`;
    }).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">Calendar is empty.</p></div>';
    document.getElementById('coachAnalytics').innerHTML = athletes.slice(0, 5).map((item, index) => `<details class="detail-card" ${index === 0 ? 'open' : ''}><summary><h3>${item.user.fullName}</h3><div class="pill">${item.latest?.performanceKpis?.readinessIndex || item.latest?.overallScore || 0}%</div></summary><div class="detail-body">${item.topIssue ? `Main issue: ${item.topIssue.metric}` : 'Awaiting first saved report.'}</div></details>`).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">Analytics will appear after athlete reports are saved.</p></div>';
    document.getElementById('accountabilityQueue').innerHTML = athletes.slice(0, 5).map((item, index) => `<details class="detail-card" ${index === 0 ? 'open' : ''}><summary><h3>${item.user.fullName}</h3><div class="pill">${item.retention.daysSinceLastAssessment ?? 'n/a'} days</div></summary><div class="detail-body">${item.retention.accountabilityState === 'at-risk' ? 'Needs follow-up now.' : 'Training cadence is healthy.'}</div></details>`).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">No accountability items yet.</p></div>';

    const targets = athletes.map((item) => `<option value="${item.user.id}">${item.user.fullName}</option>`).join('');
    document.getElementById('athleteTarget').innerHTML = targets || '<option value="">No athletes available</option>';
    document.getElementById('coachMessageFeed').innerHTML = messages.slice(0, 10).map((item) => {
      const counterpart = item.fromUserId === access.user.id ? (item.toName || 'Athlete') : (item.fromName || 'Athlete');
      return `<div class="card"><div class="row"><div><div class="mini">${counterpart}</div><h3>${item.subject}</h3><p class="muted">${item.body}</p></div><div class="mini">${new Date(item.createdAt).toLocaleString()}</div></div></div>`;
    }).join('') || '<div class="card"><p class="muted">Coach and athlete messages will appear here.</p></div>';
    document.getElementById('coachMessageForm').addEventListener('submit', (event) => {
      event.preventDefault();
      try {
        store.sendMessage({ toUserId: document.getElementById('athleteTarget').value, subject: document.getElementById('athleteSubject').value || 'Coaching note', body: document.getElementById('athleteBody').value || '' });
        window.location.reload();
      } catch (error) {
        alert(error.message || 'Unable to send coaching note.');
      }
    });

    document.getElementById('guardrailList').innerHTML = [
      `Coach access can review athlete data, but player dashboards stay scoped to each logged-in player.`,
      `Plan and trial rules still control report limits, exports, and premium surfaces.`,
      `Coach messaging, schedules, and athlete notes stay scoped to assigned players only.`
    ].map((item) => `<div class="card"><p class="muted">${item}</p></div>`).join('');

    function csvEscape(value){return `"${String(value ?? '').replace(/"/g,'""')}"`;}
    window.exportCoach = function(format){
      const payload = { summary, athletes, sessions };
      if(format === 'json'){
        const blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(payload,null,2)], { type: 'application/json' });
        const link = document.createElement('a'); link.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob); link.download = 'smartswing-coach-dashboard.json'; link.click(); return;
      }
      const rows = [['athlete','score','issue'],...athletes.map((item) => [item.user.fullName, item.latest?.overallScore || 0, item.topIssue?.metric || 'none'])];
      const csv = rows.map((row) => row.map(csvEscape).join(',')).join('\n');
      const blob = new Blob([csv], { type: 'text/csv;charset=utf-8' });
      const link = document.createElement('a'); link.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob); link.download = 'smartswing-coach-dashboard.csv'; link.click();
    };
    window.printCoachDashboard = function(){ window.print(); };
  </script>
</body>
</html>

  <script>
    const store = window.SmartSwingStore;
    const form = document.getElementById('contactForm');
    const notice = document.getElementById('contactNotice');
    const key = 'smartswing_contact_messages';

    function readMessages() {
      try {
        const raw = localStorage.getItem(key);
        return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : [];
      } catch (error) {
        return [];
      }
    }

    function writeMessages(messages) {
      localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(messages));
    }

    form.addEventListener('submit', async (event) => {
      event.preventDefault();

      const entry = {
        id: `msg_${Date.now()}`,
        name: document.getElementById('name').value.trim(),
        email: document.getElementById('email').value.trim().toLowerCase(),
        topic: document.getElementById('topic').value,
        message: document.getElementById('message').value.trim(),
        createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
      };

      if (!entry.name || !entry.email || !entry.topic || !entry.message) {
        notice.className = 'notice warn';
        notice.textContent = 'Please complete all fields before submitting.';
        return;
      }

      const messages = readMessages();
      messages.unshift(entry);
      writeMessages(messages);
      form.reset();

      if (store?.saveContactMessage) {
        try {
          const sync = await store.saveContactMessage(entry);
          if (sync?.synced) {
            notice.className = 'notice success';
            notice.textContent = `Message saved and synced to cloud at ${new Date(entry.createdAt).toLocaleString()}.`;
            return;
          }
        } catch (_) {
          // Keep local success fallback
        }
      }

      notice.className = 'notice success';
      notice.textContent = `Message saved locally at ${new Date(entry.createdAt).toLocaleString()}. Total stored messages: ${messages.length}.`;
    });
  </script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

  <meta charset="UTF-8">

  <title>SmartSwing AI Cookie Policy</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Read how SmartSwing AI uses cookies, storage, analytics, and consent choices.">
  <meta name="robots" content="index,follow">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://www.smartswingai.com/cookie-policy.html">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Sans:wght@400;500;700;800&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="./policy-pages.css">
</head>
<body>
  <main class="page">
    <header class="topbar">
      <a class="brand" href="./index.html">Smart<span>Swing</span> AI</a>
      <nav class="nav">
        <a class="btn" href="./privacy-policy.html">Privacy Policy</a>
        <a class="btn" href="./california-privacy.html">California Choices</a>
        <a class="btn-primary" href="./contact.html">Contact Us</a>
      </nav>
    </header>

    <section class="hero">
      <article class="panel">
        <div class="eyebrow">Cookies</div>
        <h1>Cookie and storage controls should be understandable, not hidden.</h1>
        <p>This Cookie Policy explains how SmartSwing AI may use cookies, local storage, pixels, analytics tools, and similar technologies on the public website and logged-in experience.</p>
      </article>
      <article class="panel">
        <h2>Categories</h2>
        <ul class="hero-list">
          <li>Strictly necessary items that support login state, security, and core service functions.</li>
          <li>Analytics items that help measure performance, errors, and product usage patterns.</li>
          <li>Preference items that remember choices such as consent or user experience settings.</li>
          <li>Marketing or personalization tools only where applicable and permitted by law.</li>
        </ul>
      </article>
    </section>

    <section class="content-grid">
      <article class="panel section-stack">
        <div class="section-copy">
          <h2>Consent and region-specific use</h2>
          <p>Consent expectations can vary by region. Users in the EEA, UK, and Switzerland may see or require consent choices before certain non-essential technologies are used. California residents may also have additional rights relating to sharing, advertising, or opt-out controls. Brazilian users may also have consent-related and information rights under the LGPD depending on the cookie or processing context.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article class="panel section-stack">
        <div class="section-copy">
          <h2>Managing choices</h2>
          <p>You can manage cookies through browser settings, delete stored data, or use the cookie banner when available. Blocking some technologies may affect sign-in persistence, analytics, or personalized product behavior. SmartSwing AI aims to avoid loading non-essential analytics on browsers that have not recorded analytics consent through the available preference flow.</p>
          <p class="meta">Effective date: March 25, 2026</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </section>

    <footer class="legal-footer">
      <div class="footer-column">

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

  <meta name="description" content="Read SmartSwing AI's copyright policy and notice process for allegedly infringing content.">
  <meta name="robots" content="index,follow">
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://www.smartswingai.com/copyright-policy.html">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Sans:wght@400;500;700;800&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="./policy-pages.css">

  <main class="page">
    <header class="topbar">
      <a class="brand" href="./index.html">Smart<span>Swing</span> AI</a>
      <nav class="nav">
        <a class="btn" href="./brand-policy.html">Brand Policy</a>
        <a class="btn" href="./contact.html">Contact Us</a>
        <a class="btn-primary" href="./user-agreement.html">User Agreement</a>
      </nav>
    </header>

    <section class="hero">
      <article class="panel">
        <div class="eyebrow">Copyright</div>
        <h1>Respect original content and report problems with enough detail to investigate them.</h1>
        <p>SmartSwing AI respects copyright and expects users to upload or share only content they have the right to use. This policy describes how to report allegedly infringing material and how SmartSwing AI may respond.</p>
      </article>
      <article class="panel">
        <h2>Notice expectations</h2>
        <ul class="hero-list">
          <li>Identify the copyrighted work claimed to be infringed.</li>
          <li>Identify the material you want reviewed with enough specificity to locate it.</li>
          <li>Provide your name, contact information, and a good-faith explanation of the claim.</li>
          <li>Confirm that the information in your notice is accurate to the best of your knowledge.</li>
        </ul>
      </article>
    </section>

    <section class="content-grid">
      <article class="panel section-stack">
        <div class="section-copy">
          <h2>How SmartSwing AI may respond</h2>
          <p>Depending on the claim, SmartSwing AI may remove or restrict access to content, contact the reporting party or the affected user, request more detail, or take further account-level action for repeated infringement or misuse.</p>
        </div>
      </article>
      <article class="panel section-stack">
        <div class="section-copy">
          <h2>Where to send notices</h2>
          <p>Use the contact page and select a copyright-related topic. Include URLs, screenshots, dates, and enough detail to identify the material at issue.</p>
          <p class="meta">Effective date: March 25, 2026</p>
        </div>
      </article>
    </section>

    <footer class="legal-footer">
      <div class="footer-column">
        <strong>Copyright support</strong>
        <p>Notices should be specific enough for SmartSwing AI to locate the material in question.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="footer-column">
        <strong>Related pages</strong>
        <div class="footer-links">

  <script src="./public-app-config.js"></script>
